zh.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: 英國諾圖波藍德阿克頓 安德 笛卡爾 費爾頓
zh.Hexham, Northumberland, England: 英國諾圖波藍德可哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: Richard de Acton於大約1282年出生在英國諾圖波藍德阿克頓 安德 笛卡爾 費爾頓。他至少有3個兒子。他在英國諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋被列為mayor 。他去世於1342年5月17日之前在英國諾圖波藍德可哈姆。

zh-hans.Hexham, Northumberland, England: 英国諾圖波藍德可哈姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard de Acton于大约1282年出生在英国諾圖波藍德阿克頓 安德 笛卡爾 費爾頓。他至少有3个儿子。他在英国諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋被列为mayor 。他去世于1342年5月17日之前在英国諾圖波藍德可哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Acton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places, especially in Shropshire and adjacent counties, named Acton. Generally, these are from Old English āc ‘oak’ + tūn ‘settlement’. In Ireland the surname has been established in Connacht since the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Richard de Acton was born about 1282, in Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, England. He had at least 3 sons. His occupation is listed as mayor  in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England. He died before 17 May 1342, in Hexham, Northumberland, England.

fr.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Richard de Acton est né environ 1282, à Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 3 fils. Sa profession de son indiquée est mayor  à Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Angleterre. Il est décédé avant 17 mai 1342, à Hexham, Northumberland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Richard de Acton wurde um 1282 in Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, England geboren. Er hatte mindestens 3 Söhne. sein Beruf ist als mayor  in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England aufgeführt. Er starb vor 17. Mai 1342 in Hexham, Northumberland, England.

ja.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: イギリスノースウンバーランドアクトン アンド オルド フエルトン
ja.Hexham, Northumberland, England: イギリスノースウンバーランドヘクスハム
ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Actonは約1282年にイギリスノースウンバーランドアクトン アンド オルド フエルトンで生まれた。彼は少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。イギリスノースウンバーランドニューカストル ウプオン トインでの職業はmayor と記録されている。彼は1342年5月17日以前にイギリスノースウンバーランドヘクスハムで死去した。

ko.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: 영국 노르스벌랜드 악턴 앤드 드 프턴
ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Acton(은)는 대략 1282년 영국 노르스벌랜드 악턴 앤드 드 프턴에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 노르스벌랜드 뉴카스틀 업 타이니에서 mayor 이었다. 그는 1342년 5월 17일 이전 영국 노르스벌랜드 헤스함에서 사망했다.

Sua profissão aparece como mayor  em Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu antes 17 de maio de 1342, em Hexham, Northumberland, Inglaterra.

ru.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: актон анд олд фелтон, нортумбирланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Richard de Acton родился около 1282 г. в местности актон анд олд фелтон, нортумбирланд, Англия. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына.  Он работал по профессии mayor  в местности нюкастл упон тин, нортумбирланд, Англия. Он умер перед 17 май 1342 г. в местности хексхам, нортумбирланд, Англия.
